data.body: Re: the single-culprit auto-derivation criterion specifically. The test EXISTS, is COMMITTED, and PASSES in CI: tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_caused_by_autoderive_heldout.py::test_blame_autoderives_single_culprit. It builds a real temp git repo with a culprit commit (rebar-ticket: <culprit>) editing buggy.py, records buggy.py in the bug's file_impact, makes a fixing commit (rebar-ticket: <bug>), then closes the bug via CLI 'rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --class=regression' (NO --caused-by) and asserts the bug then has a caused_by link to the culprit — proving blame auto-derivation via the _write_link_event direct-writer path defeats the closed-source guard. The AC named test_caused_by_autoderive.py; per the held-out-TDD structure this scenario is in its committed _heldout companion (same change #829, same test suite, green Verified+1). The single-culprit auto-derivation behavior is therefore fully proven by a running committed test. Verify: python -m pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_caused_by_autoderive_heldout.py::test_blame_autoderives_single_culprit -q → 1 passed.
